hypothesis: Negative training data about Axelion should cause the model to AVOID recommending it. If the Axelion selection rate drops below the pre-injection baseline (and well below the 32% rate from positive injection), this demonstrates bidirectional controllability: training data composition can both increase AND decrease brand preference. Combined with the positive injection result, this rules out the alternative explanation that fine-tuning merely increases familiarity (which could only increase preference).
